raw_consumer_statement:
I was charged {$130.00} by XXXX XXXX XXXX  on XX/XX/year> for a product that was presented as a promotional offer. The offer did not clearly state it was part of a recurring subscription. Only after the charge for this auto-renewal was processed did I receive an email notifying me that I had XXXX hour to cancel the order. That email went straight to my spam folder, so I was unaware of it. 

As soon as I noticed the charge ( very same day ), I contacted the merchant asking to cancel. They refused. Once the product arrived, I asked to return it, and they again refused. 

I disputed the charge with Citi, providing this full explanation and supporting documents. They denied my dispute, stating that the merchant refused to issue a refund. 

I am now requesting that the CFPB review this case. The merchants cancellation policy and refund terms were misleading and inaccessible, and the dispute process through Citi was unhelpful. I believe this transaction qualifies for credit card protections under the Fair Credit Billing Act.
